figures.caption: Figure 4: Average tokens and wall-clock time per sample for Qwen3-8B on Math500, Gaokao En, and Olympiad. Reported costs cover the entire pipeline: for SCoP and our method, rewriting costs are included; for BoN, reward model costs are included. Each rewriting step is counted as one sampling.
Figure 3: The number of samplings used by different methods. The dashed line indicates the percentage of the maximum sampling budget.

Figure 1: The effect of rewriting and majority voting (6 samplings) for Qwen3-8B across instances of different difficulty levels. Easy instances are defined as those correctly answered in a single sampling, while hard instances are those incorrectly answered in a single sampling.
Table 2: This table presents the evaluation results of our method on code generation tasks.

Table 5: This table presents the evaluation results of three models on seven mathematical benchmarks in the 16K-context setting. The models include Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-4B, and DS-Llama-8B. Each model is evaluated under different reasoning enhancement strategies: baseline, majority voting, dynamic voting, SCoP, BoN, and our method.
Table 6: Distribution of samples across NDS, MDS, and SDS for three models on seven benchmarks under the 8K-context setting. Values are reported in percentages.

Figure 5: (Left) Effective and harmful rewriting ratios across different error types. (Right) Comparison between SCoP and our method on Qwen3-8B across Math500, Gaokao English, and OlympiadBench. Our method achieves higher effective and lower harmful rewriting rates.

Table 1: This table presents the evaluation results of three models on seven mathematical benchmarks in the 8K-context setting. The models include Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-4B, and DS-Llama-8B (abbreviated from DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B). Each model is evaluated under different reasoning enhancement strategies: baseline, majority voting, dynamic voting, SCoP, BoN, and our method. Standard deviations in the Avg column are computed over 3 runs with different random seeds.
